name: Gables Pawn & Jewelry
description_short: Miami-based high-end pawnbroker specializing in luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe) and fine jewelry. Offers collateralized loans, sales, and watch repair since 1986.
description_long:
Gables Pawn & Jewelry has operated as a specialized pawnbroker in Miami since 1986, establishing itself as a niche player focused exclusively on luxury goods rather than general merchandise. Located at 1734 S. Red Road in Miami (10 minutes from Miami International Airport), the company has built a 37-year track record serving South Florida residents seeking loans and sales of high-end timepieces and jewelry.

The company offers three primary services: collateralized pawn loans ($200–$1,000,000) on watches, diamonds, and gold; buying and selling of pre-owned luxury items; and expert watch repair and refinishing. They maintain an inventory of over 200 pre-owned watches (primarily Rolex) and GIA-certified diamonds, and specialize in signed jewelry from brands like Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, Tiffany & Co., and Bulgari. Loan amounts range from small to seven figures, with immediate payment options via cash, check, or wire transfer.

What distinguishes Gables from typical pawn shops is their focus on ultra-premium items and expertise infrastructure. They employ on-site watchmakers, jewelers, and gemologists, and are members of the International Watch & Jewelry Guild (IWJG) and the Polygon Network—both recognized industry credentials. Their website emphasizes a clean, professional environment (explicitly contrasted with stereotypical pawn shop aesthetics in customer testimonials) and confidentiality. They also provide written insurance appraisals and specialize in brands like Rolex, Omega, Breitling, and Patek Philippe.

However, this is fundamentally a pawn operation: customers pledging collateral receive short-term loans at terms not disclosed on the website, and rates/terms are negotiated individually. No interest rates, loan terms, or fee structures are published. The business model is transactional and item-dependent, not suitable for consumers seeking transparent, fixed-rate financing. Customers must visit in person or call for quotes.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Specializes in ultra-high-value items ($200–$1,000,000 loans) with on-site expert appraisers (watchmaker, jeweler, gemologist)
Over 37-year operating history in same Miami location; member of International Watch & Jewelry Guild and Polygon Network
Immediate payment via cash, check, or wire transfer with no apparent waiting period
Maintains inventory of 200+ pre-owned luxury watches and GIA-certified diamonds; can also appraise for insurance
Full-service watch repair and refinishing for most brands at stated 'reasonable prices'
Professionally maintained, secure, and confidential environment explicitly designed to contrast with typical pawn shop aesthetics
Accepts a wide range of collateral: luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe, Omega), certified diamonds, signed jewelry, gold, platinum
cons: No published interest rates, fees, or loan terms—all pricing negotiated individually with no transparency
Requires in-person visit or phone consultation; no online loan calculator or rate quotes available
Specialty focus on luxury goods means unsuitable for consumers with mainstream collateral (electronics, vehicles, etc.)
Short-term pawn loan model inherently burdensome for customers unable to repay quickly; no information on renewal or default consequences
Website emphasizes brand affiliations ('Rolex, Cartier, etc.') but includes disclaimer of non-affiliation, potentially misleading
best_for: High-net-worth individuals needing emergency liquidity without selling luxury watches or jewelry
Collectors or estate liquidators buying/selling pre-owned Rolex, Cartier, or Patek Philippe watches
Owners of certified diamonds or signed fine jewelry seeking quick cash loans or fair-market sales
diagnosis: Gables Pawn & Jewelry is best for affluent consumers with luxury collateral who need short-term liquidity and value expertise and discretion over transparent pricing. The critical caveat is that this is a pawn operation—loans are collateralized, short-term, and come with unstated interest rates and terms; it is not a substitute for personal loans or credit products and should only be used when selling items is not viable.
services: Pawn loans on luxury watches, diamonds, and gold ($200–$1,000,000)
Buy pre-owned luxury watches (Rolex, Cartier, Omega, Breitling, Patek Philippe, etc.)
Sell pre-owned watches and estate jewelry from inventory
Buy signed fine jewelry (Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, Harry Winston, Tiffany & Co., Bulgari)
Buy gold, platinum, coins, and ingots (scrap or bullion)
Expert watch repair and refinishing for most brands
Written insurance appraisals by on-site gemologists
Immediate payment via cash, check, or wire transfer
Inventory browsing of 200+ pre-owned watches and GIA diamonds
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
